title: Florida key lime pie
subtitle: Sweetened-condensed-milk lime curd on a biscuit base
excerpt: The Florida key lime pie: a digestive-biscuit base, a lime-and-condensed-milk filling set by the lime acid and a brief bake, and a soft whipped-cream top. One 23 cm pie, serving eight.
sourceNotes: Borden Eagle Brand condensed-milk recipe pamphlet (1931, public domain US trade): documents the canonical key-lime-pie filling that uses sweetened condensed milk as the set agent (the lime acid chemically firms the milk solids). The Florida Keys household tradition predates refrigeration; condensed milk was the milk product that kept on the boat. The digestive-biscuit base is the British adaptation of the American graham-cracker shell.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze without the cream top; defrost in the fridge overnight; whip and add the cream on the day of serving.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Best made the day before so the filling fully sets and the lime flavour develops.

recipeTools.notes: Or crush biscuits in a bag with a rolling pin.

content.text: Key lime pie sets by chemistry as much as by heat: the citric acid in the lime juice firms the proteins in the sweetened condensed milk. A brief bake at low heat finishes the set; the long fridge chill firms it the rest of the way.
Key limes are the small, yellow-skinned variety from the Florida Keys. Standard Persian limes (the big green ones in British supermarkets) give a similar flavour; use what you have.

items.prepNote: zest and juice — about 150 ml juice

content.text: Heat the oven to 160°C fan. Blitz the {{digestive-biscuit}} to fine crumbs (or crush in a bag with a rolling pin). Mix with the melted {{unsalted-butter}}. Press evenly into a 23 cm tin, pushing up the sides 2 cm. Bake for 10 minutes to set. Cool while you make the filling.

Whisk the {{condensed-milk}} with the {{egg-yolks}} until smooth. Whisk in the zest and juice of the {{lime}}; the mixture will thicken visibly as the acid hits the milk. Pour into the cooled base. Bake for 15 minutes; the edges should be set, the centre should wobble slightly. Cool fully, then chill at least 4 hours, or overnight.
Whip the {{double-cream}} with the {{icing-sugar}} to soft peaks. Spoon or pipe over the chilled pie. Serve with a little extra lime zest on top.
